headlines.title: A major shift could soon happen in the Mag 7

headlines.title: Fed dissenters explain 'no' votes, saying they disagreed with hinting next move would be a cut
headlines.desc: Federal Reserve officials who voted this week against the post-meeting statement said they didn't think it was appropriate to signal that the next interest rate move would be lower.

headlines.title: Oil prices rise after US and Iran exchange fire in Hormuz strait

headlines.title: Coinbase posts steep first-quarter loss after slide in crypto prices; shares fall 4%

headlines.title: South Africa’s top court revives impeachment inquiry against president
headlines.desc: The scandal centres on a large sum of foreign currency stolen from President Cyril Ramaphosa’s farmhouse in 2020.

headlines.title: Rubio says Iranian boats firing at Americans will be ‘blown up’

headlines.title: How and when can Arsenal or Man City win the Premier League?

headlines.title: ASEAN leaders adopt measures to ease economic pain caused by Iran war
headlines.desc: The bloc currently imports more than half of its crude oil from the Middle East.

headlines.title: Libya’s largest oil refinery halts operations during fighting
headlines.desc: Zawiya refinery shut down in 'precautionary measure' as emergency declared following explosions and gunfire nearby.

headlines.title: Intel’s stock achieves a never-before-seen feat as Apple enthusiasm builds
headlines.desc: Intel is in the midst of transforming its business, with shares rising 14% on Friday following news of a new Apple manufacturing partnership.

headlines.title: ‘The S&P 500 seems to be doing particularly well’: I’m 66. Is this a good time to invest $100,000 in the stock market?

headlines.title: Gold has climbed back above a key trend line. Are more gains on the way?
headlines.desc: Gold broke above an important short-term technical level on Friday — a sign that the precious metal may be starting to regain momentum after a sluggish stretch since the start of the Iran conflict.

headlines.title: The Federal Reserve is quickly running out of reasons to cut interest rates
headlines.desc: Friday's jobs report provided evidence that the central bank's larger concern is a cost of living that is getting increasingly hard to bear.

headlines.title: Fed Governor Waller says Iran war and labor market risks are keeping central bank on hold
